titleStmt.title: Letter from W.A.F. Barry to Sir Matthew Nathan, 18 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: W.A.F. Barry

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Application letter from W.A.F. Barry to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) concerning the position of Crown Solictor for Kings County. In the 1911 census, W.A.F. Barry is listed as 18 years of age and working as a solicitor apprentice.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary’s Office Registered papers, CSO-RP-1916-5615-10272

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
John's Place Birr 18th June 1916 Sir, I have the honor to ask you to submit my name
as a candidate for the office of Crown Solicitor for the
King's County vacated by the recent death of my father,
Mr. Richard Fitzwilliam Barry L.L.B., after about twenty
years service. I served my apprenticeship under my father at Birr;
since my admission as a solicitor my father and I
carried on the civil business of the office together
and I have assisted him in his official work as
Crown Solicitor; and I may mention that since my
father's health first began to fail namely about
eighteen months ago I have had the main share
in carrying on the office work in connection with
his position as Crown Solicitor. I trust that these facts will weigh in my favor in
the consideration of my fitness for the position. I have the honor to be
Sir
Your obedient servant W. A. F. Barry The Under Secretary Dublin Castle
